sentence: If she had studied harder, she ______ the exam. (third conditional)
explanation: Third conditional result: <would have> + past participle.

sentence: I wish I ______ more time to travel. (wish + past)
explanation: After <wish>, use past simple to express unreal present desires.

sentence: She had her car ______ at the garage. (causative)
explanation: Causative <have something done>: have + object + past participle.

sentence: Not only ______ she speak French, but she also speaks Italian. (inversion)
explanation: After <Not only> at the start, use inverted word order: <does she>.

sentence: It was the manager ______ made the final decision. (cleft sentence)
explanation: Cleft sentence: <It was... who/that> emphasizes the subject.

sentence: The project fell ______ because of lack of funding. (phrasal verb: fail)
explanation: The phrasal verb <fall through> means to fail to happen as planned.

sentence: We need to iron ______ the remaining issues before launch.
explanation: The phrasal verb <iron out> means to resolve problems or difficulties.

sentence: She ______ up with a brilliant idea during the meeting.
explanation: The phrasal verb <come up with> means to think of or produce an idea.

sentence: He ______ down the job offer because of the low salary.
explanation: The phrasal verb <turn down> means to reject or refuse.

sentence: The company is phasing ______ the old software gradually.
explanation: The phrasal verb <phase out> means to discontinue something gradually.

sentence: Don't put all your eggs in one ______. Diversify your investments.
explanation: The idiom <don't put all your eggs in one basket> means don't risk everything on one option.

sentence: The elephant in the room is the obvious problem that nobody wants to ______.
explanation: The idiom <the elephant in the room> refers to an obvious problem everyone ignores.

sentence: She's barking up the wrong tree. She's looking in the wrong ______.
explanation: The idiom <barking up the wrong tree> means pursuing a mistaken approach.

sentence: He decided to bite the bullet and ______ the difficult conversation.
explanation: The idiom <bite the bullet> means to face something difficult with courage.

sentence: That's the last straw. I can't ______ it anymore.
explanation: The idiom <the last straw> means the final problem that makes a situation unbearable.

sentence: The nominali______ of verbs is common in academic writing.
explanation: Adding <-ation> to <nominalize> forms the noun <nominalization>.

sentence: His reckless______ caused the accident.
explanation: Adding <-ness> to <reckless> forms the noun <recklessness>.

sentence: The prefix 'mis-' in 'misunderstand' means ______.
explanation: The prefix <mis-> means wrongly or badly: <misunderstand> = understand wrongly.

sentence: Adding '-ive' to 'create' gives us ______, meaning having the quality of creating.
explanation: The suffix <-ive> forms adjectives: <creative> = having the quality of creating.

sentence: The prefix 'over-' in 'overestimate' means ______.
explanation: The prefix <over-> means too much or excessively: <overestimate> = estimate too highly.

sentence: If I ______ you, I would accept the offer. (transform: advice using conditional)
explanation: Second conditional for advice: <If I were you> (subjunctive form).

sentence: The report ______ being prepared when the power went out. (transform to passive continuous)
explanation: Past continuous passive: <was being> + past participle.

sentence: Had I ______ about the delay, I would have left earlier. (inverted conditional)
explanation: Inverted third conditional: <Had I known> = If I had known.

sentence: It ______ that the company will announce layoffs. (transform: 'People say the company...' → impersonal passive)
explanation: Impersonal passive: <It is said that...> reports general beliefs.

sentence: She ______ to have left the country. (transform: 'People believe she left' → passive report)
explanation: Passive reporting: subject + <is believed> + to have + past participle.
